title: Nihon Kohden Life Scope G Series - ECG Lead Failure
description: Guide for troubleshooting ECG lead failures on Nihon Kohden Life Scope G Series monitors.

steps.title: Check the patient connection
steps.instructions: Ensure all ECG leads are correctly attached to the patient. Verify that limb and chest electrodes are placed according to standard protocol. Why: Incorrect placement or loose electrodes are the most common cause of lead failures.

steps.title: Inspect the ECG cables and connectors
steps.instructions: Look for broken, frayed, or bent lead wires. Ensure connectors are clean and securely inserted into the monitor. Why: Damaged cables or poor connections can cause intermittent or failed signals.

steps.title: Check electrode quality
steps.instructions: Confirm that electrodes are not dried out or expired. Replace with fresh electrodes if needed. Why: Poor electrode adhesion or conductivity prevents accurate ECG readings.

steps.title: Monitor channel selection and configuration
steps.instructions: Ensure the monitor is set to display the correct ECG channel (I, II, III, etc.). Verify the monitor hasn’t been set to “Lead Off” detection sensitivity too high. Why: Configuration issues may mimic a lead failure even if the leads are fine.

steps.title: Test with a known good patient simulator or test lead set
steps.instructions: Connect the monitor to a test simulator or verified working leads. Why: Confirms whether the problem is patient-side or monitor-side.

steps.title: Inspect for patient movement or interference
steps.instructions: Check if excessive movement, tremors, or electrosurgical devices could be interfering with the ECG signal. Why: Motion artifact can trigger lead failure alarms.

CCR.Complaint: ECG lead failure alarm on Life Scope G monitor, no waveform displayed.
CCR.Cause: Lead cables verified, electrodes replaced, patient connection confirmed, alarm persists—external issues ruled out.
CCR.Resolution: Monitor removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for internal module evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Specific lead(s) failing (RA, LA, LL, V1–V6)
Indicator lights or error codes displayed
Presence of motion artifact or interference

title: Nihon Kohden Life Scope G Series - SpO₂ Reading Errors
description: Guide for troubleshooting SpO₂ reading errors on Nihon Kohden Life Scope G Series monitors

steps.title: Check the SpO₂ probe connection
steps.instructions: Ensure the probe is fully inserted into the correct port. Inspect for bent or damaged pins. Poor connection can prevent the device from reading signals accurately.

steps.title: Inspect the probe for damage
steps.instructions: Look for frayed wires, cracks, or discoloration on the sensor. A damaged probe can give erroneous readings or fail entirely.

steps.title: Verify proper placement on the patient
steps.instructions: Ensure the sensor is correctly positioned (finger, earlobe, or neonatal site as applicable). Remove nail polish or obstructions that can interfere with light transmission. Confirm the site is warm and perfused; cold or poor circulation can cause unstable readings.

steps.title: Check for patient movement or external interference
steps.instructions: Ask the patient to remain still if possible. Ensure the probe cable is not rubbing against bed rails or other equipment, which can create artifacts.

steps.title: Swap with a known good probe
steps.instructions: If available, connect a functional, compatible SpO₂ probe to verify if the reading normalizes. Confirms whether the issue is probe-related versus device-related.

steps.title: Verify monitor settings
steps.instructions: Check alarm limits, averaging times, and signal quality indicators. Incorrect settings may trigger false alarms or unstable readings.

steps.title: Power cycle the monitor
steps.instructions: Turn the monitor off, wait 30 seconds, and restart. Resets the sensor detection circuitry and clears transient errors.

CCR.Complaint: Monitor intermittently displays SpO₂ errors and fails to provide a reading.
CCR.Cause: Probe connector loose and sensor slightly damaged; site was cold and patient moving.
CCR.Resolution: Repositioned probe, confirmed connection, replaced with functional sensor, monitored readings stabilized. If errors persist, device sent to bench.

helpfulDetails: Device response after swapping probe or power cycling

title: Nihon Kohden Life Scope G Series - NIBP Calibration Issues
description: Troubleshooting guide for inaccurate or failed NIBP readings on the Nihon Kohden Life Scope G Series monitor.

steps.title: Check Power and Device Readiness
steps.instructions: Ensure the monitor is powered on and not in standby mode. Confirm that other vitals are displayed correctly to rule out broader system faults. Why: A partially booted device or low battery may interfere with NIBP measurements.

steps.title: Inspect the NIBP Cuff
steps.instructions: Verify the cuff is the correct size for the patient. Look for tears, worn material, or leaks. Ensure the cuff hose connection is secure at both ends (cuff → tubing → monitor). Why: A damaged or improperly sized cuff is the most common cause of calibration errors.

steps.title: Examine the Tubing and Connectors
steps.instructions: Check for kinks, pinches, or sharp bends in the tubing. Look for air leaks by gently inflating the cuff manually and listening for hissing. Ensure all connectors are fully seated. Why: Tubing leaks or improper connections can prevent accurate pressure readings.

steps.title: Confirm Proper NIBP Setup
steps.instructions: Verify that the correct measurement interval and mode are selected. Ensure the patient’s arm is at heart level and still during measurement. Why: Improper setup can trigger calibration errors even if the hardware is fine.

steps.title: Check Device Calibration Status
steps.instructions: Review the monitor’s NIBP calibration log if available. If the device indicates the need for a manual calibration, follow manufacturer-approved procedures. Why: Some calibration routines are time-dependent; missing or overdue calibrations may trigger errors.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Monitor
steps.instructions: Turn the monitor off, wait 30 seconds, and turn it back on. Retest the NIBP measurement. Why: Temporary software glitches can sometimes cause calibration errors.

CCR.Complaint: Monitor repeatedly shows NIBP calibration error and fails to start measurement.
CCR.Cause: Cuff inspected and tubing verified with no leaks. Device software and settings checked. Calibration log indicates error persists.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for manufacturer evaluation. Temporary monitor used for patient care.

helpfulDetails: Tubing inspected for leaks or kinks
Final device status: Out of Service

title: Nihon Kohden Life Scope G Series - Battery Not Charging
description: Guide to troubleshoot battery charging issues on Nihon Kohden Life Scope G Series monitors.

steps.title: Verify AC Power Source
steps.instructions: Confirm the wall outlet is supplying power using a known working device. Check that outlet is not switched off or connected to a GFCI that has tripped. Why: No power at the source is the simplest cause of charging failure.

steps.title: Inspect AC Power Cord and Plug
steps.instructions: Examine the cord for cuts, fraying, or bent pins. Ensure the plug is fully inserted into the monitor and outlet. Why: A damaged or loose cord prevents proper charging.

steps.title: Check the Battery Module Seating
steps.instructions: Remove and reinstall the battery, ensuring it clicks securely into place. Inspect battery terminals for dirt, corrosion, or debris. Why: Improper seating or poor terminal contact prevents charging.

steps.title: Observe Monitor’s Charging Indicator
steps.instructions: Plug in the monitor with the battery installed and watch the charging light or on-screen indicator. Record any error messages displayed. Why: Confirms whether the monitor detects the battery and attempts to charge.

steps.title: Swap with a Known Good Battery
steps.instructions: If available, insert a known working Life Scope G Series battery and attempt charging. Why: Determines if the issue is battery-specific or monitor-related.

steps.title: Inspect for Monitor Settings or Lockouts
steps.instructions: Check monitor configuration to ensure battery charging is enabled (some units may limit charging in certain modes). Why: Software or mode restrictions may prevent charging.

CCR.Complaint: Monitor battery will not charge when connected to AC power.
CCR.Cause: Battery terminals inspected; cord and outlet verified; monitor does not indicate charging; battery replacement with known good unit confirms monitor failure.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Outlet tested with another device: pass/fail
Final device status: Out of Service if unresolved

title: Nihon Kohden Life Scope G Series - Touchscreen Unresponsive
description: Guide to troubleshoot unresponsive touchscreen on the Life Scope G Series patient monitor, emphasizing external checks before escalation.

steps.title: Ensure Device Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the monitor is not actively monitoring a patient. If the monitor is in use, move the patient to a backup device first. Why: Patient safety is the priority; avoid troubleshooting during active monitoring.

steps.title: Check Power Status
steps.instructions: Verify AC power connection and battery level. Ensure the monitor is powered on and shows normal startup sequence. Why: Touchscreen may appear unresponsive if device is not fully powered.

steps.title: Inspect Touchscreen Surface
steps.instructions: Clean the screen using a soft, lint-free cloth and approved monitor cleaning solution. Remove any protective films, adhesive labels, or debris. Why: Dirt, moisture, or residue can interfere with capacitive touch sensing.

steps.title: Restart the Monitor
steps.instructions: Power off the monitor completely, wait 30 seconds, and power on. Why: Software or touchscreen drivers may reset, restoring functionality.

steps.title: Check for Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect for cracks, loose bezels, or signs of impact. Why: Physical damage can prevent proper touchscreen operation and may require repair.

steps.title: Verify Peripheral Connections
steps.instructions: Disconnect external cables (NIBP, SpO₂, ECG leads, USB devices) and check if touchscreen responds. Why: Conflicting signals from peripherals can sometimes interfere with touchscreen response.

steps.title: Check for System Alerts or Software Locks
steps.instructions: Look for on-screen messages like “Touchscreen locked” or software update prompts. Follow manufacturer instructions if the screen is locked. Why: Some modes disable touch input temporarily.

CCR.Complaint: Touchscreen does not respond to input; monitor buttons and menus unresponsive.
CCR.Cause: Screen contaminated with residue and software may have been in a locked or unresponsive state.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned touchscreen, verified power and peripheral connections, attempted restart; monitor remains unresponsive → removed from service and sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: AC power confirmed and battery charged
No unusual sounds or lights observed
Final device status: Out of Service

title: Nihon Kohden BSM-6000 Series - ECG Lead Connection Errors
description: This guide assists Clinical Engineering in troubleshooting ECG lead connection errors on Nihon Kohden BSM-6000 series monitors.

steps.title: Ensure patient safety first
steps.instructions: Move the patient to another monitor if continuous monitoring is critical. Never troubleshoot ECG leads on an active patient without alternate monitoring.

steps.title: Inspect electrode placement and skin preparation
steps.instructions: Confirm electrodes are on clean, dry skin. Reposition any electrodes that appear loose or poorly adhered. Replace old or dried-out electrodes. Correct placement ensures proper waveform acquisition and reduces lead errors.

steps.title: Check ECG lead cables
steps.instructions: Verify all lead cables are fully seated in both the monitor and electrode clips. Look for visible damage: frays, cracks, broken clips, or bent pins. Swap leads with a known-good set to rule out cable defects.

steps.title: Verify lead configuration in the monitor
steps.instructions: Confirm the monitor is set to the correct lead type (3-lead, 5-lead, 12-lead if applicable). Misconfigured lead selection can produce false errors or abnormal waveforms.

steps.title: Inspect connectors and port cleanliness
steps.instructions: Ensure ECG input ports on the monitor are clean and free of debris. Check for bent or corroded pins. If connectors appear dirty, gently clean with isopropyl alcohol and a lint-free swab.

steps.title: Restart the monitor
steps.instructions: Power cycle the monitor to reset any transient software or detection errors. Observe whether the error persists after restart.

CCR.Complaint: ECG leads intermittently fail to display waveform; alarms indicate lead error.
CCR.Cause: Leads were loose, some electrodes poorly adhered, and one lead cable showed minor fraying.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced defective lead cable, reapplied electrodes with proper skin prep, and confirmed stable waveform acquisition.

helpfulDetails: Verified lead type and monitor settings
Checked ECG input ports for damage or debris
Final device status: stable waveform observed, no further errors

title: Nihon Kohden BSM-6000 Series - Display or Touchscreen Malfunction
description: Guide for troubleshooting display or touchscreen malfunctions on Nihon Kohden BSM-6000 series patient monitors, focusing on external causes before internal failure.

steps.title: Check the power source
steps.instructions: Verify that the monitor is plugged into a live outlet. Confirm the AC adapter or power cable is securely connected. Test the outlet with another device to rule out outlet failure. Why: Power instability is a common cause of display issues.

steps.title: Inspect the battery (if applicable)
steps.instructions: Ensure battery is properly seated and charged. Replace with a known-good battery if available. Why: Low or failing battery can cause flickering or blank screens.

steps.title: Check for external damage
steps.instructions: Inspect the touchscreen for cracks, scratches, or liquid exposure. Look for loose or damaged connectors at the display interface. Why: Physical damage can prevent proper touchscreen response.

steps.title: Perform a soft reset
steps.instructions: Power off the monitor completely, wait 30 seconds, and power back on. Observe if the display initializes correctly. Why: Software or temporary glitches often resolve with a reset.

steps.title: Verify settings and brightness
steps.instructions: Adjust screen brightness via the monitor menu if visible. Ensure no “sleep” or “screen saver” mode is active. Why: Misconfigured display settings can mimic hardware failure.

steps.title: Check environmental factors
steps.instructions: Ensure the monitor is not exposed to extreme temperatures or electromagnetic interference. Why: Environmental stress can affect touchscreen responsiveness.

steps.title: Try alternate inputs
steps.instructions: If touchscreen is unresponsive, check if the monitor responds to button controls. Why: Confirms whether issue is limited to touchscreen interface.

CCR.Complaint: Touchscreen is unresponsive, display flickers intermittently, and some buttons do not respond.
CCR.Cause: Confirmed power outlet functional, AC adapter and battery seated correctly, soft reset performed, environmental factors normal. Touchscreen remains unresponsive.
CCR.Resolution: Monitor removed from service, labeled ‘Out of Service’, sent to Biomedical Engineering bench for internal evaluation/repair.

helpfulDetails: AC adapter or battery swapped: Yes/No
Display flicker, blank, or distorted: Observed behavior
External damage or liquid exposure: Observed/None
Final device status: Out of Service

title: Nihon Kohden BSM-6000 Series - Alarm Not Sounding / Configuration Problems
description: Guide for Clinical Engineering to troubleshoot alarms that are not sounding or misconfigured on Nihon Kohden BSM-6000 series monitors.

steps.title: Verify Power and Battery
steps.instructions: Confirm the monitor is plugged in and turned on. Check battery level if running on battery; low battery can disable alarms. Why: Alarm circuits may be disabled in low-power conditions.

steps.title: Check Volume Settings
steps.instructions: Access the monitor’s alarm settings menu. Ensure master volume and individual alarm volumes are not muted or set too low. Why: Simple misconfiguration is a frequent cause of silent alarms.

steps.title: Verify Alarm Limits
steps.instructions: Review configured alarm thresholds for each vital sign (HR, SpO₂, NIBP, etc.). Ensure values are within realistic ranges to trigger an alarm. Why: If limits are set outside expected patient values, alarms will not activate.

steps.title: Inspect External Connections
steps.instructions: Confirm ECG leads, SpO₂ sensors, NIBP cuffs, and other probes are connected properly. Look for disconnected or damaged cables. Why: Alarms often rely on valid input signals; disconnected sensors can suppress alarms.

steps.title: Check Audio Output
steps.instructions: Ensure the internal speaker is unobstructed and functional. If an external speaker is connected, verify connections and settings. Why: Sound may be routed incorrectly or blocked by physical obstructions.

steps.title: Perform a System Reset
steps.instructions: Power cycle the monitor following manufacturer instructions. Reset user-configurable settings to default if necessary. Why: Software glitches or temporary misconfigurations can prevent alarms from sounding.

CCR.Complaint: Monitor alarm not sounding during abnormal vitals.
CCR.Cause: Volume set to minimum and NIBP cuff disconnected; master alarm muted.
CCR.Resolution: Adjusted volume settings, reconnected NIBP cuff, verified alarm function with test signal.

title: Nihon Kohden BSM-6000 Series - SpO₂ Sensor Errors
description: Guide for troubleshooting SpO₂ sensor errors on Nihon Kohden BSM-6000 series monitors.

steps.title: Check Sensor Connection
steps.instructions: Ensure the SpO₂ sensor is firmly connected to the monitor port. Loose or partially connected sensors can prevent readings.

steps.title: Inspect the Sensor and Cable
steps.instructions: Look for visible damage, kinks, frays, or exposed wires. Confirm that the sensor type is compatible with the BSM-6000 series.

steps.title: Verify Sensor Placement
steps.instructions: Confirm proper placement on the patient (finger, toe, or earlobe depending on the sensor). Ensure the sensor site is clean, dry, and free from nail polish, excessive movement, or ambient light interference.

steps.title: Swap with a Known Good Sensor
steps.instructions: If possible, replace the current sensor with a known working sensor. This helps identify whether the issue is the sensor or the monitor input.

steps.title: Check Monitor Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm the SpO₂ module is enabled in the monitor settings. Verify alarm thresholds and averaging times are appropriate for the patient.

steps.title: Assess Patient Factors
steps.instructions: Poor perfusion, tremors, or cold extremities can prevent accurate readings. If safe, try an alternative measurement site.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Monitor
steps.instructions: If external checks fail, power off the monitor, wait 30 seconds, and restart. Some transient errors can be cleared with a simple reboot.

CCR.Complaint: Monitor shows SpO₂ error, no readings available.
CCR.Cause: Sensor cable appeared intact, but swapping with a known working sensor restored readings. Original sensor likely defective.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced SpO₂ sensor. Verified accurate readings on patient. Monitor returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Outlet tested (if applicable for powered sensors)
Any unusual sounds or heat from the sensor module

title: Nihon Kohden BSM-6000 Series - NIBP Measurement Errors or Failures
description: Guide for Clinical Engineering to troubleshoot NIBP measurement errors on Nihon Kohden BSM-6000 monitors.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is not dependent on the monitor for real-time monitoring during troubleshooting. Move the patient to an alternative monitor if necessary.

steps.title: Check NIBP Cuff Application
steps.instructions: Verify the cuff is the correct size for the patient. An undersized or oversized cuff can cause errors. Ensure the cuff is positioned correctly on the limb (usually upper arm, at heart level) and wrapped snugly without excessive tightness. Inspect for kinks, folds, or damage in the cuff material.

steps.title: Inspect Tubing and Connections
steps.instructions: Examine the NIBP tubing for cracks, leaks, or disconnections. Confirm the tubing is securely connected to both the cuff and the monitor’s NIBP port. If possible, swap tubing with a known working set to rule out tubing failure.

steps.title: Check for Patient Movement or Interference
steps.instructions: Ensure the patient is relaxed and not moving the limb during measurement. Movement can cause failed readings or errors. Remove tight clothing or jewelry that may compress the cuff during measurement.

steps.title: Verify Monitor Settings and Measurement Mode
steps.instructions: Confirm the NIBP measurement mode (automatic, manual, continuous) is correctly configured. Ensure alarm thresholds and intervals are set appropriately. Restart the monitor if software glitches are suspected.

steps.title: Observe Monitor Indicators
steps.instructions: Watch for error codes displayed on the monitor. Reference the user guide for error code definitions (e.g., cuff off, excessive movement, low battery). Check for unusual sounds, flashing indicators, or error messages that may indicate hardware issues.

CCR.Complaint: User reports NIBP measurement errors or inability to initiate measurement.
CCR.Cause: Cuff was found loosely wrapped and tubing slightly kinked, causing failed readings.
CCR.Resolution: Repositioned cuff, ensured proper tubing connection, and performed test measurement. Device function restored.

helpfulDetails: Outlet tested: N/A for NIBP, but verify monitor powers on.
Cables swapped: Tubing swapped with a known good set.
Alarm behavior: Monitor displayed error code “Cuff Off” during initial test.
Indicator lights: NIBP indicator flashed red during error.
Unusual sounds: Pump attempted inflation but released quickly.
Final device status: Working after cuff/tubing adjustment; patient monitored safely.

title: Nihon Kohden ECG-1500 Series Electrocardiograph (EKG) Machine - Lead Fail or Poor Signal Acquisition
description: Troubleshooting lead fail messages, noisy ECG waveforms, or poor signal acquisition due to electrode, cable, or connection issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the ECG is being used for diagnosis, confirm the patient is stable and move to another functioning ECG machine if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Confirm the lead fail message or poor waveform quality and observe which leads are affected, signal behavior, and artifacts.

steps.title: Check Electrode Placement and Condition
steps.instructions: Ensure proper placement, replace poor electrodes, and prepare skin for good contact.

steps.title: Inspect Lead Wires and Patient Cable
steps.instructions: Check for damage, secure connections, and test for intermittent faults by gently moving cables.

steps.title: Swap with Known Good Accessories
steps.instructions: Replace lead wires and patient cable to determine if accessories are the cause.

steps.title: Check Input Ports on the Device
steps.instructions: Inspect connector for debris or damage and ensure proper seating.

steps.title: Evaluate Environmental Interference
steps.instructions: Reduce motion and nearby electrical interference to improve signal quality.

steps.title: Perform System Self-Test (If Available)
steps.instructions: Run built-in diagnostics to identify internal faults.

CCR.Complaint: ECG-1500 showing lead fail and poor waveform quality during patient acquisition.
CCR.Cause: Defective patient lead wire set causing intermittent signal loss.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced lead wires with known good set; verified normal waveform acquisition across all leads.

title: Nihon Kohden ECG-1500 Series Electrocardiograph (EKG) Machine - Thermal Printer Errors or Paper Jam
description: Troubleshooting printer errors, paper jams, and poor print quality on the Nihon Kohden ECG-1500 Series due to paper handling or obstruction issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot the printer while actively acquiring a diagnostic ECG on a patient. If printing is required for care, move to another device first. Confirm patient care is not interrupted.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to print a test ECG or stored record. Observe error messages on display, no paper feed or partial feed, paper stopping mid-print, and faded or blank output.

steps.title: Check Paper Supply and Type
steps.instructions: Open the printer compartment and inspect the paper roll. Ensure paper is present, confirm correct thermal paper type, and verify paper is not wrinkled, damp, or damaged.

steps.title: Verify Correct Paper Orientation
steps.instructions: Ensure thermal paper is loaded correctly with the thermal side facing the print head. Flip the roll if unsure and test print.

steps.title: Inspect for Paper Jam or Debris
steps.instructions: Remove the paper roll and inspect the path. Look for torn paper, remove debris, and ensure rollers move freely.

steps.title: Reload Paper Properly
steps.instructions: Reinstall the paper roll evenly, feed paper straight through the exit slot, and close the printer door securely.

steps.title: Check Printer Door and Latch
steps.instructions: Ensure the printer cover is fully closed and latched with no errors.

steps.title: Perform a Test Print
steps.instructions: Run a test ECG or internal print. Confirm smooth feed, consistent quality, and no errors.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off, wait 10–15 seconds, and power back on to reset the printer.

CCR.Complaint: EKG machine not printing and displaying paper jam error during use.
CCR.Cause: Paper roll installed incorrectly and partial paper obstruction found in feed path.
CCR.Resolution: Cleared jam, reloaded paper correctly, and verified proper print function.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (returned to service or sent for repair)

title: Nihon Kohden ECG-1500 Series Electrocardiograph (EKG) Machine - Network or EMR Export Failure
description: Failure to transmit ECG data to EMR due to network, configuration, or connectivity issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not delay patient care due to export issues. Complete ECG acquisition and ensure results are available locally or printed if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Confirm whether export fails completely or intermittently, check for error messages, and verify if ECGs are saving locally but not transmitting.

steps.title: Check Network Connection (Physical or Wireless)
steps.instructions: Verify Ethernet cable connection, check link lights, and confirm Wi-Fi connection if applicable.

steps.title: Test Network Availability
steps.instructions: Confirm other devices are working on the network and restart the ECG unit if needed.

steps.title: Verify Device Network Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm IP address, subnet, gateway, and DNS settings match the hospital network configuration.

steps.title: Confirm EMR / Server Configuration
steps.instructions: Verify server IP/hostname, port, protocol, and patient/worklist integration settings.

steps.title: Check Worklist or Patient Data Requirements
steps.instructions: Ensure patient data is correct, verify worklist functionality, and test export with a new patient entry.

steps.title: Attempt Manual Export or Alternate Method
steps.instructions: Retry sending ECG, attempt USB export or printing, and determine if the issue is intermittent.

steps.title: Check for Error Messages or Logs
steps.instructions: Review system messages such as connection failed, server not reachable, or transmission error.

steps.title: Power Cycle and Reinitialize Network
steps.instructions: Restart the ECG machine to reset the network stack and clear temporary faults.

CCR.Complaint: ECG machine not transmitting studies to EMR; staff reports repeated send failures.
CCR.Cause: Network cable found disconnected from wall port, preventing communication.
CCR.Resolution: Reconnected Ethernet cable and verified successful ECG transmission to EMR.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (returned to service or sent out)

title: Nihon Kohden ECG-1500 Series Electrocardiograph (EKG) Machine - Display or Keypad Failure
description: Unresponsive screen, blank display, dim screen, or keypad not registering inputs due to power, connection, or user interface issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the device is in use, stop acquisition and move the patient to another EKG machine before troubleshooting. Expected: Patient monitoring continues on a functional device. Why it matters: Loss of display or input prevents safe operation and data acquisition.

steps.title: Verify Power to the Unit
steps.instructions: Check if the unit is powered on. Confirm power indicator lights are on. Press the power button to ensure proper startup. Observe for screen activity (logo, backlight, or sounds). Expected: Device powers on with visible display activity. Why it matters: A blank screen may be a power issue, not a display failure.

steps.title: Check AC Power and Battery Status
steps.instructions: Inspect external power sources. Ensure AC cord is securely plugged into both the device and outlet. Test the outlet with another device. Check battery indicator status on screen (if visible). Expected: Device operates on AC power or shows battery charge status. Why it matters: Low or failed battery can cause screen dimming or failure to boot.

steps.title: Adjust Display Brightness/Contrast Settings
steps.instructions: If the screen is on but difficult to see: Adjust brightness using available controls or menu. View screen from different angles. Look for faint images indicating backlight issues. Expected: Screen becomes visible and readable. Why it matters: Apparent display failure may be due to low brightness or backlight settings.

steps.title: Inspect for Physical Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions: Check the screen and keypad surface. Look for cracks, liquid intrusion, or residue. Check for stuck or worn keypad buttons. Clean surface if contamination is present. Expected: Buttons move freely and screen is intact. Why it matters: Physical damage or debris can prevent proper input or visibility.

steps.title: Test Keypad Functionality
steps.instructions: Attempt to use multiple keys. Press several buttons and observe for response. Listen for audible feedback (beeps). Check if only certain keys are non-responsive. Expected: Keys respond consistently with input feedback. Why it matters: Partial failure may indicate keypad wear or connection issue.

steps.title: Perform a Device Restart
steps.instructions: Reboot the unit to clear potential software issues. Power off the device completely. Wait 10–15 seconds. Power back on and observe startup behavior. Expected: Device boots normally with responsive display and keypad. Why it matters: Temporary software faults can affect interface responsiveness.

steps.title: Check for External Accessories Interference
steps.instructions: Disconnect any non-essential peripherals. Remove USB devices, network cables, or accessories. Restart the device without accessories connected. Expected: Device functions normally without interference. Why it matters: External devices can occasionally cause system instability.

steps.title: Observe for Startup Behavior
steps.instructions: During reboot, note: Does the screen remain blank? Does it partially display then freeze? Are there any error tones or indicators? Expected: Normal startup sequence with full display function. Why it matters: Helps differentiate between display hardware vs system-level failure.

CCR.Complaint: EKG machine screen blank and keypad not responding during patient setup.
CCR.Cause: Failed display/backlight or keypad interface after power and restart checks confirmed no external issue.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service and sent to Biomed for repair and evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Presence of physical damage or contamination

title: Nihon Kohden ECG-1500 Series Electrocardiograph (EKG) Machine - Battery Not Charging
description: EKG unit not charging, running only on battery, or shutting down unexpectedly due to power supply, battery, or connection issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the device is in use, transfer the patient to another EKG machine before troubleshooting. Expected: Patient monitoring continues uninterrupted. Why it matters: Power loss can interrupt ECG acquisition and documentation.

steps.title: Verify Power Source and Outlet
steps.instructions: Check the wall outlet using another device or outlet tester. Confirm outlet is live. Try a known working outlet. Expected: Outlet provides consistent power. Why it matters: Dead or switched outlets are a common external cause.

steps.title: Inspect AC Power Cord and Adapter
steps.instructions: Examine the power cord and adapter for damage or loose connections. Ensure cord is firmly seated in both wall and device. Check for fraying, bent prongs, or loose adapter connections. Swap with a known good cord if available. Expected: Secure connection with no visible damage. Why it matters: Faulty cords prevent charging even when plugged in.

steps.title: Check Power Indicator Lights
steps.instructions: Observe device indicators when plugged into AC power. Look for charging LED or power indicator. Note if device powers on with AC connected. Expected: Charging indicator illuminates or device runs on AC power. Why it matters: Confirms whether the unit is recognizing external power.

steps.title: Verify Battery Seating
steps.instructions: If accessible externally, ensure the battery is properly installed. Power off device. Remove and reseat battery (if user-accessible). Inspect contacts for dirt or damage. Expected: Battery sits firmly with clean contact points. Why it matters: Poor contact prevents charging and power transfer.

steps.title: Check Battery Age and Condition
steps.instructions: Determine battery history and performance. Ask staff about runtime duration. Note if battery drains quickly or fails to hold charge. Expected: Battery should hold charge for normal operation time. Why it matters: Worn batteries may not accept or retain a charge.

steps.title: Test Device on AC Power Only
steps.instructions: Operate the unit while plugged in. Remove battery (if possible) and run on AC. Observe stability and functionality. Expected: Device operates normally on AC power. Why it matters: Helps isolate battery vs internal charging circuit issue.

steps.title: Inspect for Heat, Smell, or Damage
steps.instructions: Check for signs of electrical failure. Look for overheating near power input. Smell for burnt odor. Listen for unusual noises. Expected: No abnormal heat, smell, or sound. Why it matters: Indicates potential internal failure requiring escalation.

CCR.Complaint: EKG machine not charging and shuts off when unplugged.
CCR.Cause: Failed battery unable to accept or hold charge.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced battery and verified normal charging operation on AC power.

helpfulDetails: Any heat, smell, or visible damage
Final device status (in service or sent for repair)

title: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 Ventilator - Alarm: High PIP
description: Troubleshooting guide for a Nihon Kohden NKV-330 ventilator High PIP alarm caused by elevated airway pressure, circuit restriction, incorrect settings, blocked sensing lines, or breath delivery problems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess the patient immediately, confirm ventilation and oxygenation, and move the patient to an approved alternate ventilation method if safe ventilation cannot be confirmed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the ventilator displays High PIP and observe when the alarm occurs, including during inspiration, after setting changes, after circuit replacement, after patient movement, or during coughing or suctioning.

steps.title: Check the Patient First
steps.instructions: Have clinical staff assess for coughing, secretions, biting the tube, bronchospasm, tube obstruction, patient-ventilator fighting, or changes in lung compliance.

steps.title: Check Ventilator Control Settings
steps.instructions: Review tidal volume or pressure control setting, inspiratory time, flow, PEEP, mode, and rise time or flow delivery behavior. Compare settings to the patient order.

steps.title: Check Alarm Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm the upper PIP alarm limit is appropriate for the patient and was not set too close to normal peak pressure values.

steps.title: Inspect the Patient Interface
steps.instructions: Check the endotracheal tube, trach tube, mask, or other patient interface for kinks, compression, obstruction, secretions, mispositioned tubing, or patient biting.

steps.title: Inspect the Breathing Circuit
steps.instructions: Inspect the inspiratory and expiratory limbs for kinked tubing, blocked filters, water accumulation, crushed tubing, incorrect assembly, or obstructed exhalation path.

steps.title: Check the Exhalation Port
steps.instructions: Confirm the exhalation port or exhalation path is not blocked, wet, contaminated, occluded, or incorrectly connected.

steps.title: Check the Pressure Sensor Line
steps.instructions: Inspect the pressure sensor line for water droplets, kinks, clogging, loose connection, disconnection, or incorrect routing.

steps.title: Check the Flow Sensor Line
steps.instructions: Inspect the flow sensor line and connections for water droplets, kinks, obstruction, loose fittings, incorrect connection, or contamination.

steps.title: Replace Suspect External Accessories
steps.instructions: Substitute known-good compatible accessories such as the breathing circuit, filter, pressure sensor line, flow sensor line, or patient interface to isolate external restrictions.

steps.title: Calibrate the Pressure Sensor
steps.instructions: Remove the ventilator from active patient use and perform pressure sensor calibration according to facility procedure and manufacturer guidance.

steps.title: Calibrate the Breathing Circuit
steps.instructions: Use the correct circuit setup, confirm all required ports and sensors are connected, and run breathing circuit calibration.

steps.title: Verify Operation After Corrections
steps.instructions: Test the ventilator on a test lung, observe multiple breaths, and confirm stable PIP values, proper cycling, and no repeated High PIP alarm.

CCR.Complaint: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 ventilator alarming High PIP during inspiration. Staff reported elevated peak inspiratory pressure and repeated breath termination to PEEP or atmospheric pressure.
CCR.Cause: Checked patient condition, control settings, alarm settings, patient interface, breathing circuit, exhalation port, pressure sensor line, and flow sensor line. Water, kink, or blockage was found in the pressure sensing path and corrected.
CCR.Resolution: Cleared obstruction, replaced affected line, performed pressure sensor and breathing circuit calibration, tested on test lung, and confirmed High PIP alarm did not return. Ventilator returned to service.

title: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 Ventilator - Alarm: LOW PIP
description: Troubleshooting guide for a LOW PIP alarm on a Nihon Kohden NKV-330 ventilator when peak inspiratory pressure is at or below the lower alarm limit.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is being ventilated safely. Have clinical staff assess chest rise, breath sounds, oxygen saturation, end-tidal CO2 if available, airway status, and overall condition. If ventilation is questionable, provide manual ventilation or move the patient to another ventilator before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the ventilator display shows LOW PIP. Observe when the alarm occurs and whether peak inspiratory pressure is consistently at or below the lower alarm limit.

steps.title: Check the Patient Interface for a Large Leak
steps.instructions: Inspect the mask, endotracheal tube, tracheostomy tube, adapters, suction ports, caps, and cuff condition. Correct any loose connection, poor seal, disconnected tubing, or leaking cuff.

steps.title: Check Control Settings and Alarm Settings
steps.instructions: Review ventilation mode, pressure settings, volume settings, flow settings, PEEP, and alarm limits. Confirm the lower PIP alarm limit is appropriate for the patient and ordered therapy. Only clinical staff should adjust therapy settings.

steps.title: Inspect the Breathing Circuit for Leaks or Disconnections
steps.instructions: Check the inspiratory limb, expiratory limb, humidifier connections, filters, adapters, Y-piece, water traps, and exhalation components. Reseat or replace loose, cracked, damaged, disconnected, or leaking components.

steps.title: Check the Pressure Sensor Line
steps.instructions: Trace the pressure sensor line and check for loose connections, cracked tubing, water droplets, kinks, clogging, blockage, or incorrect routing. Clear, reconnect, or replace the line as appropriate.

steps.title: Check the Flow Sensor Line
steps.instructions: Inspect the flow sensor line for leaks, water accumulation, kinks, clogging, blockage, loose fittings, or incorrect connection. Replace or correct the line if there is any doubt.

steps.title: Check for Water, Kinks, or Blockage in the Circuit
steps.instructions: Inspect the breathing circuit, pressure sensor line, and flow sensor line for condensation, dependent loops, kinks, clogging, or blocked tubing. Drain water traps and replace contaminated or obstructed tubing according to facility procedure.

steps.title: Perform Pressure Sensor and Breathing Circuit Calibration
steps.instructions: Remove the ventilator from patient use. Perform facility-approved pressure sensor calibration and breathing circuit calibration using the correct circuit setup. Verify calibration completes successfully.

steps.title: Isolate Accessory vs Device Failure
steps.instructions: Test with a known-good breathing circuit, pressure sensor line, and flow sensor line if safe and appropriate. Run the ventilator on a test lung and observe whether peak inspiratory pressure rises properly and whether the LOW PIP alarm returns.

CCR.Complaint: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 ventilator alarming LOW PIP. Peak inspiratory pressure reported at or below the lower alarm limit during ventilation.
CCR.Cause: Found large leak at patient circuit connection causing low measured airway pressure.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated circuit connection, inspected breathing circuit, pressure sensor line, and flow sensor line. Verified no leaks, kinks, water, clogging, or blockage. Alarm cleared during test lung verification. Ventilator returned to service.

title: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 Ventilator - Alarm: High PEEP
description: Troubleshooting guide for the Nihon Kohden NKV-330 High PEEP alarm when measured PEEP is more than 5 hPa above the EPAP/CPAP setting, including patient safety, circuit restriction checks, sensor line inspection, calibration, escalation, and CCR documentation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient’s respiratory status immediately, have clinical staff assess the patient and airway, provide alternate ventilation if needed, and remove the ventilator from patient use if the alarm persists or pressure delivery appears abnormal.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the ventilator is displaying the High PEEP alarm, compare measured PEEP to the EPAP/CPAP setting, and note when the alarm occurs.

steps.title: Check the Patient and Airway
steps.instructions: Have clinical staff assess for coughing, patient-ventilator fighting, breath stacking, secretion buildup, airway obstruction, or compliance changes.

steps.title: Check Control and Alarm Settings
steps.instructions: Verify EPAP/CPAP, ventilation mode, pressure settings, recent setting changes, and alarm limits.

steps.title: Inspect the Patient Interface
steps.instructions: Inspect the mask, airway adapter, ET tube connection, trach connection, or other patient interface for obstruction, moisture, secretions, crushed tubing, or misconnections.

steps.title: Inspect the Breathing Circuit
steps.instructions: Check the full breathing circuit for kinked tubing, water accumulation, blocked filters, crushed tubing, incorrect circuit setup, obstructed expiratory limb, or misconnected components.

steps.title: Check the Exhalation Port
steps.instructions: Confirm the exhalation port is present, correct, and unobstructed. Check for water, contamination, secretions, coverings, or incorrect placement.

steps.title: Check the Pressure Sensor Line
steps.instructions: Inspect the pressure sensor line for water droplets, kinks, clogging, blockage, loose connections, or disconnected tubing.

steps.title: Check the Flow Sensor Line
steps.instructions: Inspect the flow sensor line for water droplets, kinks, clogging, blockage, loose fittings, or damaged tubing.

steps.title: Calibrate the Pressure Sensor and Breathing Circuit
steps.instructions: Remove the ventilator from patient use, install the proper test setup, perform pressure sensor calibration and breathing circuit calibration, and confirm calibration passes.

steps.title: Recheck Operation After Corrective Action
steps.instructions: Run the ventilator on the appropriate test setup, observe measured PEEP, confirm it remains close to the EPAP/CPAP setting, and verify the High PEEP alarm does not return.

CCR.Complaint: NKV-330 ventilator reported High PEEP alarm. Measured PEEP was more than 5 hPa above the EPAP/CPAP setting during ventilation.
CCR.Cause: Water droplets and partial blockage were found in the expiratory circuit and pressure sensor line, causing elevated measured PEEP.
CCR.Resolution: Removed affected circuit components, replaced contaminated tubing, verified exhalation port was clear, completed pressure sensor and breathing circuit calibration, and confirmed the High PEEP alarm did not return during testing.

title: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 Ventilator - Alarm: LOW PEEP
description: Troubleshooting guide for the LOW PEEP alarm on a Nihon Kohden NKV-330 ventilator when measured PEEP is too low compared with the EPAP or CPAP setting.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is being adequately ventilated, assess oxygen saturation and chest rise, involve clinical staff, and move the patient to backup ventilation if the LOW PEEP alarm is persistent or associated with poor ventilation.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the ventilator displays LOW PEEP, observe when the alarm occurs, compare measured PEEP to the EPAP or CPAP setting, and check for related pressure, leak, flow, or circuit alarms.

steps.title: Translate the Alarm Into the Real-World Problem
steps.instructions: Treat the alarm as a possible pressure loss or pressure measurement problem. Focus first on leaks, loose connections, patient interface fit, circuit setup, and sensor-line problems.

steps.title: Check the Patient Interface for Leaks
steps.instructions: Inspect the mask, tube, trach connection, or other patient interface for poor seal, loose fit, disconnected tubing, damaged connectors, or excessive leak.

steps.title: Check Control Settings and Alarm Settings
steps.instructions: Review EPAP, CPAP, PEEP-related settings, ventilation mode, and alarm limits. Confirm settings match the clinical plan and were not changed accidentally.

steps.title: Inspect the Breathing Circuit for Leaks
steps.instructions: Inspect inspiratory and expiratory tubing, adapters, filters, humidifier connections, exhalation components, and fittings for leaks, loose connections, damage, or incorrect assembly.

steps.title: Check the Pressure Sensor Line
steps.instructions: Inspect the pressure sensor line for loose connection, leak, kink, water droplets, blockage, or disconnection. Correct or replace the line if needed.

steps.title: Check the Flow Sensor Line
steps.instructions: Inspect the flow sensor line for loose connection, leak, kink, water, blockage, or clogging. Correct or replace the line if needed.

steps.title: Check for Water or Condensation in the Circuit
steps.instructions: Inspect the breathing circuit, pressure sensor line, and flow sensor line for water or condensation. Drain or replace affected components according to facility practice.

steps.title: Calibrate the Pressure Sensor and Breathing Circuit if Needed
steps.instructions: After correcting external issues, perform the appropriate facility-approved pressure sensor and breathing circuit calibration procedure.

steps.title: Confirm the Repair
steps.instructions: Run the ventilator on an approved test lung or verification setup, confirm measured PEEP remains appropriate compared with EPAP or CPAP, and verify the LOW PEEP alarm does not return.

CCR.Complaint: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 ventilator reported LOW PEEP alarm. Measured PEEP was too low compared with the EPAP/CPAP setting during ventilation.
CCR.Cause: Found leak at patient interface or breathing circuit connection causing expiratory pressure loss and LOW PEEP alarm. Pressure and flow sensor lines were checked for leaks, water, kinks, and blockage.
CCR.Resolution: Corrected circuit/interface leak, verified settings and alarm limits, inspected sensor lines, and confirmed measured PEEP remained stable during test operation. Device returned to service.

title: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 Ventilator - Alarm: HIGH VT
description: Troubleshooting guide for the Nihon Kohden NKV-330 HIGH VT alarm when measured tidal volume is at or above the upper alarm limit, including patient safety, settings review, leak checks, circuit inspection, pressure sensor line checks, flow sensor line inspection, and escalation steps.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is being adequately ventilated. Have clinical staff assess the patient immediately. If ventilation is unstable, transfer the patient to another ventilator or approved manual ventilation method before continuing troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the ventilator display shows the HIGH VT alarm and that measured tidal volume is at or above the upper alarm limit. Check for related pressure, leak, flow, or circuit alarms and observe when the alarm occurs.

steps.title: Check the Patient and Clinical Condition
steps.instructions: Have clinical staff confirm whether the measured tidal volume makes sense for the patient. Check patient size, ventilation mode, prescribed settings, spontaneous breathing, coughing, agitation, and whether the upper VT alarm limit is appropriate.

steps.title: Check Control Settings and Alarm Settings
steps.instructions: Verify tidal volume, pressure support, pressure control, inspiratory pressure, EPAP, PEEP, and mode settings as applicable. Confirm the upper VT alarm limit is not set too low and review recent setting changes with clinical staff.

steps.title: Check for Large Leaks at the Patient Interface
steps.instructions: Inspect the mask, endotracheal tube, trach connection, adapter, and patient wye. Look for loose connections, poor mask seal, disconnected fittings, or damaged adapters.

steps.title: Inspect the Breathing Circuit for Leaks
steps.instructions: Inspect inspiratory and expiratory limbs, tubing connections, fittings, adapters, and circuit components. Look for cracks, loose fittings, damaged tubing, disconnected components, or incorrect circuit type.

steps.title: Check the Pressure Sensor Line
steps.instructions: Confirm the pressure sensor line is securely connected. Inspect for cracks, loose fittings, kinks, moisture, or contamination. Replace the line if damaged or questionable.

steps.title: Check the Flow Sensor Line
steps.instructions: If a flow sensor is in use, inspect the flow sensor line for water droplets, kinks, clogging, blockage, loose connections, and leaks. Replace the line or flow sensor if contamination, damage, or blockage is found.

steps.title: Replace and Recalibrate the Flow Sensor if Needed
steps.instructions: Install a known-good compatible flow sensor if needed and perform the required flow sensor calibration according to approved procedure. Confirm measured tidal volume returns to expected values and the HIGH VT alarm clears.

steps.title: Confirm Resolution Before Returning to Use
steps.instructions: Verify the HIGH VT alarm no longer occurs, measured tidal volume is stable and reasonable, and related leak, pressure, flow, or circuit alarms are absent. Document parts swapped, calibration performed, and final device status.

CCR.Complaint: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 ventilator reported HIGH VT alarm during use. Measured tidal volume was at or above the upper alarm limit.
CCR.Cause: Inspected patient interface, breathing circuit, pressure sensor line, and flow sensor line. Found flow sensor line partially obstructed with moisture, causing inaccurate tidal volume measurement.
CCR.Resolution: Removed moisture and replaced affected flow sensor components, recalibrated flow sensor, verified measured tidal volume returned to expected range, and confirmed HIGH VT alarm did not recur. Returned ventilator to service.

title: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 Ventilator - Alarm: LOW VT
description: Troubleshooting guide for the Nihon Kohden NKV-330 LOW VT alarm when measured tidal volume is at or below the lower alarm limit, including patient safety, settings, leak checks, circuit inspection, sensor line checks, flow sensor review, escalation, and CCR documentation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient's condition immediately, have clinical staff assess ventilation status, and provide alternate ventilation if the patient is not being adequately ventilated. Do not continue troubleshooting while safe ventilation is uncertain.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the displayed alarm reads LOW VT, observe when it occurs, and verify measured tidal volume is at or below the lower alarm limit.

steps.title: Check Ventilator Mode, Control Settings, and Alarm Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm the prescribed ventilation mode and review tidal volume, pressure support, pressure control, inspiratory pressure, EPAP/PEEP, backup rate, and the lower tidal volume alarm limit.

steps.title: Check for Patient Interface Leak
steps.instructions: Inspect the mask, ET tube, trach connection, or other patient interface for loose connections, poor fit, cuff leak, disconnected tubing, or open accessory ports.

steps.title: Inspect the Breathing Circuit
steps.instructions: Check the full breathing circuit for loose fittings, cracked tubing, disconnected humidifier chambers, open ports, damaged adapters, water accumulation, kinks, obstruction, or compression.

steps.title: Check the Pressure Sensor Line
steps.instructions: Confirm the pressure sensor line is connected securely and inspect for cracks, loose fittings, water, kinks, clogging, or blockage.

steps.title: Check the Flow Sensor Line
steps.instructions: Confirm the flow sensor line is connected properly and inspect for leaks, water, kinks, clogging, blockage, damaged tubing, loose connectors, or contamination.

steps.title: Inspect the Flow Sensor if Used
steps.instructions: Inspect the flow sensor for water, secretions, contamination, damage, kinks, clogging, or blockage. Confirm correct orientation, replace questionable parts, and recalibrate if required.

steps.title: Recheck After Correcting External Issues
steps.instructions: Reconnect the circuit, confirm sensor lines are secure, verify measured tidal volume returns above the lower alarm limit, and confirm the LOW VT alarm clears.

steps.title: Remove From Service if Unresolved
steps.instructions: If the alarm persists after external checks, remove the ventilator from service, label it Out of Service, and send it for repair or bench evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 ventilator reported LOW VT alarm during use. Measured tidal volume was at or below the lower alarm limit.
CCR.Cause: Found leak at patient interface, loose breathing circuit connection, water or blockage in flow sensor line, contaminated flow sensor, or alarm limit set above expected delivered tidal volume. If unresolved, document as persistent LOW VT alarm after external causes were ruled out.
CCR.Resolution: Checked patient safety, verified LOW VT alarm, reviewed control and alarm settings, inspected patient interface, breathing circuit, pressure sensor line, flow sensor line, and flow sensor. Corrected external issue and confirmed alarm cleared, or removed ventilator from service for repair evaluation.

title: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 Ventilator - Alarm: HIGH RRTOT
description: Troubleshooting guide for the Nihon Kohden NKV-330 ventilator HIGH RRTOT alarm when total respiratory rate is at or above the upper alarm limit.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is being assessed by clinical staff, verify ventilation is adequate, and use an alternate ventilator or approved ventilation method if the patient is unstable or the ventilator cannot be trusted.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the ventilator display shows HIGH RRTOT, observe when the alarm occurs, and compare the displayed RRtot value to the configured upper respiratory rate alarm limit.

steps.title: Check the Patient First
steps.instructions: Have clinical staff assess whether the patient is actually breathing rapidly and look for distress, coughing, anxiety, secretions, fever, or increased work of breathing.

steps.title: Check the Alarm Limit Setting
steps.instructions: Review the upper respiratory rate alarm limit and have clinical staff confirm it is appropriate for the patient condition and ventilation mode.

steps.title: Check Ventilator Control Settings
steps.instructions: Verify ventilation mode, set respiratory rate, pressure support, trigger sensitivity, inspiratory time, and backup ventilation settings.

steps.title: Check for Auto-Triggering
steps.instructions: Observe breath indicators and waveform behavior, check for false triggering, water movement, circuit leaks, loose connections, or excessive trigger sensitivity.

steps.title: Inspect the Breathing Circuit and Patient Interface
steps.instructions: Inspect the patient interface, ET tube connection, mask, circuit tubing, filters, humidifier connections, and exhalation path for leaks, condensation, kinks, obstruction, damage, or loose connections.

steps.title: Check Pressure and Flow Sensor Lines
steps.instructions: Inspect pressure and flow sensor lines for water droplets, kinks, clogging, blockage, disconnection, or leaks, and replace questionable tubing or accessories as needed.

steps.title: Replace Questionable Accessories
steps.instructions: Replace suspected breathing circuit components, patient interface, flow sensor, or related tubing and recheck whether the HIGH RRTOT alarm clears.

steps.title: Confirm Resolution
steps.instructions: Confirm RRtot is below the upper alarm limit, verify the alarm does not return, and have clinical staff confirm the patient remains appropriately supported.

CCR.Complaint: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 ventilator reported HIGH RRTOT alarm during use. Total respiratory rate was at or above the upper alarm limit.
CCR.Cause: Alarm behavior, patient condition, alarm limit, ventilator settings, circuit condition, patient interface, pressure sensor line, and flow sensor line were evaluated. Issue was traced to patient rapid breathing, inappropriate alarm limit, auto-triggering, circuit leak, water in sensing line, or suspected breath detection problem.
CCR.Resolution: Verified HIGH RRTOT alarm, confirmed patient safety, inspected settings, circuit, patient interface, pressure sensor line, and flow sensor line. Corrected external issue if found. If alarm persisted, removed ventilator from service and sent for bench evaluation or repair.

title: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 Ventilator - Alarm: Low RRtot
description: Troubleshooting guide for the Nihon Kohden NKV-330 Low RRtot alarm when total respiratory rate is at or below the lower alarm limit, including patient safety checks, alarm setting review, leak checks, circuit inspection, and sensor line troubleshooting.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Respond to the Low RRtot alarm immediately. Confirm with clinical staff that the patient is being ventilated adequately. If ventilation is questionable, move the patient to another approved ventilator or provide manual ventilation before troubleshooting the device.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the ventilator displays Low RRtot. Check when the alarm occurs and compare the displayed total respiratory rate to the lower RRtot alarm limit.

steps.title: Check the Patient and Ventilation Mode
steps.instructions: Have clinical staff assess the patient's respiratory effort and ventilation status. Confirm the ventilation mode, backup rate, and spontaneous breathing expectations match the patient's condition.

steps.title: Review Control Settings and Alarm Settings
steps.instructions: Check the ventilator mode, set respiratory parameters, and lower RRtot alarm limit. Confirm the alarm limit is appropriate for the patient and clinical setup.

steps.title: Check for Patient Interface Leaks
steps.instructions: Inspect the patient interface, mask seal, ET tube or trach connection, patient wye, adapters, and fittings for leaks or loose connections.

steps.title: Inspect the Breathing Circuit
steps.instructions: Trace the breathing circuit from the ventilator to the patient. Check for loose connections, cracked tubing, disconnected limbs, incorrect assembly, damaged adapters, or water accumulation.

steps.title: Check the Pressure Sensor Line
steps.instructions: Inspect the pressure sensor line for loose connections, leaks, kinks, blockage, water droplets, condensation, or pinched tubing. Reconnect, clear, or replace the line as appropriate.

steps.title: Check the Flow Sensor Line
steps.instructions: Inspect the flow sensor line for water droplets, kinks, clogging, blockage, loose connections, contamination, or incorrect routing. Replace questionable components as needed.

steps.title: Replace and Recalibrate the Flow Sensor if Needed
steps.instructions: If flow sensor problems are suspected, replace the flow sensor according to facility practice and perform the required calibration. Confirm calibration passes and the alarm clears.

steps.title: Confirm Stable Operation
steps.instructions: After corrections, observe the ventilator under stable conditions. Confirm RRtot is appropriate, the Low RRtot alarm clears, and no related alarms recur.

CCR.Complaint: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 ventilator reported Alarm: Low RRtot during ventilation. Staff reported total respiratory rate at or below the lower alarm limit.
CCR.Cause: Inspection found possible breath detection issue related to alarm setting mismatch, patient interface leak, breathing circuit leak, pressure sensor line issue, or flow sensor line contamination/blockage.
CCR.Resolution: Verified patient safety, confirmed Low RRtot alarm, reviewed control and alarm settings, inspected patient interface, breathing circuit, pressure sensor line, and flow sensor line. Flow sensor was replaced and recalibrated as needed. Ventilator was removed from service for repair evaluation when alarm persisted after external checks.

title: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 Ventilator - Alarm: APNEA
description: Troubleshooting guide for the Nihon Kohden NKV-330 ventilator Alarm: APNEA when apnea time reaches or exceeds the upper alarm limit and no breathing activity is detected within the set alarm time.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Respond immediately. Confirm the patient is being assessed by clinical staff, verify chest rise, patient effort, oxygen saturation, airway status, and overall condition, and use approved backup ventilation if the patient is not breathing adequately.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the displayed alarm is Alarm: APNEA. Observe whether it occurs during spontaneous breathing, SPONT-PS mode, PPV mode, after a settings change, or during circuit handling.

steps.title: Confirm the Ventilation Mode and Apnea Ventilation Setting
steps.instructions: Verify whether the ventilator is in SPONT-PS or PPV mode and whether Apnea Ventilation is set to Off. Confirm with clinical staff whether apnea backup ventilation should be enabled.

steps.title: Check the Apnea Alarm Limit
steps.instructions: Review the upper apnea alarm limit and confirm it is clinically appropriate for the patient breathing pattern, mode, and current care plan.

steps.title: Check the Patient Interface
steps.instructions: Inspect the mask, ET tube, trach connection, or other patient interface for loose connection, disconnection, large leak, poor seal, patient movement, or obstruction.

steps.title: Check the Breathing Circuit Connection
steps.instructions: Inspect all circuit connections, inspiratory and expiratory limbs, tubing, fittings, and exhalation path for leaks, disconnection, water accumulation, kinks, compression, or blockage.

steps.title: Check Pressure and Flow Sensing Lines
steps.instructions: Inspect pressure and flow sensing lines for water droplets, kinks, blockage, loose connection, incorrect connection, cracks, or damaged tubing.

steps.title: Check the Flow Sensor If Installed
steps.instructions: Confirm the flow sensor is correctly installed and free of contamination, moisture, blockage, damage, or incorrect orientation. Replace or recalibrate if required.

steps.title: Check for Accessory or Setup-Related Causes
steps.instructions: Swap known-good external accessories one at a time if available and approved, including the patient circuit, patient interface, flow sensor, pressure sensor line, flow sensor line, and humidifier or HME setup.

steps.title: Confirm the Alarm Clears Under Safe Test Conditions
steps.instructions: Use an approved test lung or facility-approved test setup to confirm breath delivery, breath detection, and that the apnea alarm clears and does not return.

CCR.Complaint: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 ventilator reported Alarm: APNEA during use. Staff stated apnea time reached the alarm limit and the ventilator alarmed for no detected breathing activity.
CCR.Cause: Apnea alarm condition confirmed. Checked ventilation mode, Apnea Ventilation setting, apnea alarm limit, patient interface, breathing circuit, pressure sensor line, flow sensor line, and flow sensor. Found disconnected patient circuit connection preventing reliable breath detection.
CCR.Resolution: Reconnected and secured breathing circuit. Verified circuit integrity with approved test setup and confirmed ventilator detected breaths properly. Alarm cleared and did not return during functional check. Device returned to service.

title: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 Ventilator - Alarm: CO2 Sensor Fault
description: Troubleshooting the CO2 Sensor Fault alarm on the Nihon Kohden NKV-330 ventilator when CO2 monitoring may be unreliable due to sensor damage, deterioration, or cord failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is being monitored appropriately, notify clinical staff that the CO2 Sensor Fault alarm is active, and use alternate patient assessment methods or backup monitoring if needed. If CO2 monitoring is clinically required, use a verified replacement sensor or another verified monitoring setup.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the ventilator displays Alarm: CO2 Sensor Fault. Observe whether it occurs at startup, after sensor connection, during ventilation, or intermittently when the cable is moved. Check whether CO2 values are missing, unstable, inaccurate, or not updating.

steps.title: Check the CO2 Sensor Connection
steps.instructions: Verify the CO2 sensor is fully connected, disconnect and reconnect it firmly, inspect for loose fit, bent pins, damaged connectors, or contamination, and gently move the cable near the connector while watching for intermittent alarm behavior.

steps.title: Inspect the CO2 Sensor and Cord
steps.instructions: Inspect the sensor body for cracks, impact damage, fluid contamination, or wear. Inspect the full cord length for cuts, kinks, crushed sections, exposed conductors, stretched insulation, or strain relief damage.

steps.title: Check for Sensor Contamination or Improper Setup
steps.instructions: Verify the sensor is installed correctly. Check for moisture, condensation, secretions, or contamination around the sensor or airway adapter. Replace any contaminated or questionable airway adapter if applicable.

steps.title: Swap With a Known-Good CO2 Sensor
steps.instructions: Remove the suspected CO2 sensor and install a known-good compatible CO2 sensor. Confirm the connection is secure and observe whether the CO2 Sensor Fault alarm clears.

steps.title: Replace the CO2 Sensor if the Alarm Clears
steps.instructions: If the known-good sensor resolves the alarm, remove the defective CO2 sensor from service, install a verified replacement, confirm the alarm clears, and verify CO2 monitoring is functioning normally.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Fault Is Sensor-Related or Device-Related
steps.instructions: If the alarm remains with a known-good sensor, test the original sensor on another compatible setup if available, test the NKV-330 with another known-good sensor, and inspect the ventilator-side sensor port for damage, contamination, or bent contacts.

CCR.Complaint: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 ventilator displaying Alarm: CO2 Sensor Fault. CO2 monitoring unavailable or unreliable during use.
CCR.Cause: CO2 sensor fault confirmed. Sensor cord inspected and alarm cleared after replacing the CO2 sensor with a known-good sensor.
CCR.Resolution: Removed defective CO2 sensor from service. Installed verified replacement CO2 sensor. Confirmed alarm cleared and CO2 monitoring returned to normal operation. Ventilator returned to service.

title: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 Ventilator - Alarm: CO2 CHECK SENSOR CO2
description: Troubleshooting guide for the Nihon Kohden NKV-330 CO2 CHECK SENSOR alarm when CO2 sensor light intensity is insufficient and EtCO2 monitoring may be unreliable.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is stable and being ventilated appropriately. Do not rely on questionable EtCO2 readings while the CO2 Check Sensor alarm is active. Use alternate CO2 monitoring or move the patient to backup support if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm the ventilator display shows the reported CO2 CHECK SENSOR alarm. Observe when the alarm appears and whether EtCO2 values are missing, unstable, or inaccurate.

steps.title: Inspect the CO2 Sensor Connection
steps.instructions: Check that the CO2 sensor cable is fully connected. Inspect the connector for looseness, cracked housing, bent pins, contamination, or cable strain. Gently reseat the connector if needed.

steps.title: Check Sensor Seating on the Adapter or Cuvette
steps.instructions: Confirm the CO2 sensor is properly clipped or seated on the airway adapter or cuvette. Remove and reinstall the sensor to confirm correct alignment.

steps.title: Inspect the Airway Adapter or Cuvette
steps.instructions: Inspect the adapter or cuvette for secretions, condensation, blood, medication residue, clouding, scratches, cracks, or incorrect adapter type. Replace the adapter or cuvette if needed.

steps.title: Check the CO2 Sensor Optical Window
steps.instructions: Inspect the CO2 sensor optical window for moisture, residue, dirt, or physical damage. Clean only according to the CO2 sensor kit instructions.

steps.title: Replace the Adapter or Cuvette and Retest
steps.instructions: Install a known-good compatible CO2 airway adapter or cuvette, reconnect the sensor, and observe whether the alarm clears.

steps.title: Test With a Known-Good CO2 Sensor Kit
steps.instructions: If available, connect a known-good compatible CO2 sensor kit with the correct adapter or cuvette. Confirm whether EtCO2 values return and the alarm clears.

steps.title: Escalate if the Alarm Persists
steps.instructions: If the alarm continues after external checks and accessory replacement, remove the ventilator from service, label it Out of Service, and send it for repair or bench evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 ventilator displayed Alarm: CO2 CHECK SENSOR CO2 sensor light intensity is insufficient, and EtCO2 monitoring was unavailable or unreliable.
CCR.Cause: CO2 sensor optical path issue suspected. Sensor seating, cable connection, airway adapter/cuvette condition, and sensor window were checked for contamination or damage. Alarm persisted after external checks, indicating likely CO2 sensor kit failure or ventilator-side CO2 detection issue.
CCR.Resolution: Removed ventilator from service and labeled Out of Service for bench evaluation. Recommended testing with known-good CO2 sensor kit and compatible adapter/cuvette before further repair escalation.

title: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 Ventilator - Alarm: CO2 Cell Disconnect
description: Troubleshooting guide for the CO2 Cell Disconnect alarm on the Nihon Kohden NKV-330 ventilator when the TG-980P airway adapter is disconnected or not properly detected by the CO2 sensor kit.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Verify the patient’s respiratory status directly, confirm ventilation is being delivered appropriately, use alternate monitoring if CO2 readings are unavailable or questionable, and notify clinical staff that CO2 monitoring may be interrupted until the alarm is resolved.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the ventilator is displaying Alarm: CO2 Cell Disconnect and verify that the alarm occurs while using the TG-980P CO2 sensor setup. Note whether the alarm appears at startup, after circuit movement, after patient repositioning, after adapter changes, or intermittently during ventilation.

steps.title: Inspect the Airway Adapter Connection
steps.instructions: Check that the airway adapter is fully connected to the CO2 sensor kit. Remove and reconnect the adapter if it appears loose, partially seated, or misaligned, then observe whether the alarm clears.

steps.title: Check for Adapter Misalignment or Poor Seating
steps.instructions: Inspect the airway adapter and CO2 sensor interface for misalignment, incomplete insertion, loose fit, cracked plastic, damaged retention points, or contamination. Reconnect the adapter without twisting or forcing the connection.

steps.title: Check for Circuit Pulling or Strain
steps.instructions: Inspect the breathing circuit, sensor placement, and patient connection for tension. Reposition or support the circuit so the CO2 sensor and airway adapter remain stable during normal movement.

steps.title: Inspect the CO2 Sensor Kit and Cable
steps.instructions: Check the TG-980P CO2 sensor kit and cable for visible damage, cuts, kinks, pinched sections, loose strain relief, or damage near the sensor head. Confirm the cable is securely connected to the ventilator or related module.

steps.title: Replace the Airway Adapter
steps.instructions: Install a known-good compatible airway adapter, reconnect it to the CO2 sensor kit, allow the ventilator to recognize the accessory, and observe whether the CO2 Cell Disconnect alarm clears.

steps.title: Test With a Known-Good CO2 Sensor Kit
steps.instructions: If available, connect a known-good compatible TG-980P CO2 sensor kit and airway adapter. Verify the cable connection and adapter seating, then observe whether the alarm returns.

steps.title: Verify Alarm Resolution Before Return to Use
steps.instructions: Confirm the CO2 Cell Disconnect alarm is no longer active, verify stable CO2 monitoring if clinically expected, gently move the circuit and sensor assembly to check for intermittent disconnects, and confirm no new CO2 sensor alarms appear.

CCR.Complaint: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 ventilator displaying Alarm: CO2 Cell Disconnect while using TG-980P CO2 sensor kit. CO2 monitoring unavailable due to reported airway adapter disconnect.
CCR.Cause: Airway adapter was not fully connected to the CO2 sensor kit. Alarm cleared after adapter was properly seated and CO2 monitoring was verified.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated airway adapter on TG-980P CO2 sensor kit, verified secure connection, confirmed CO2 Cell Disconnect alarm cleared, and checked for stable CO2 monitoring. Device returned to service.

title: Nihon Kohden NKV-330 Ventilator - Alarm: CO2 BAROMETRIC SENSOR FAULT
description: Troubleshooting guide for CO2 BAROMETRIC SENSOR FAULT on the Nihon Kohden NKV-330 ventilator, including patient safety, ambient pressure checks, CO2 sensor kit inspection, replacement, and escalation steps.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not rely on questionable CO2 monitoring for patient assessment. Confirm the patient is monitored by another approved method and move the patient to a backup device if accurate CO2 monitoring is required.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact alarm displayed is CO2 BAROMETRIC SENSOR FAULT. Note whether it occurs during startup, after connecting the CO2 sensor kit, during ventilation, or intermittently.

steps.title: Check Ambient Pressure Conditions
steps.instructions: Confirm the ventilator is being used in a normal clinical environment. If available, verify ambient pressure is within the expected range of 500 hPa to 1060 hPa.

steps.title: Check the CO2 Sensor Kit Connection
steps.instructions: Inspect, disconnect, and reconnect the CO2 sensor kit. Confirm the connector is fully seated and free of bent pins, contamination, damage, or moisture.

steps.title: Inspect the CO2 Sensor Kit and Cable
steps.instructions: Check the sensor kit and cable for cracked housing, damage, kinks, strain, fluid contamination, or evidence of being dropped or pulled. Replace the CO2 sensor kit if damaged.

steps.title: Check for Moisture or Contamination
steps.instructions: Inspect the CO2 sensor area, adapter, cuvette, and connection points for moisture or residue. Replace contaminated disposables and allow approved reusable components to dry per facility procedure.

steps.title: Power Cycle and Recheck the Alarm
